changes.detail: All five mandatory fields present: work_id=Q-2, branch=feature/questionnaire-visual-redesign, risk_level=HIGH, execution_model=TWO_PHASE_START_FINISH, change_type=BEHAVIOUR. BEHAVIOUR is correct — visual layer changes constitute changes to rendered output. HIGH is conservative for a frontend-only styling sprint with no Intelligence Core involvement; GPT has authority over this classification. Consequence: dual approval required before merge.
File exists on main. Contains the Q-1 implementation (895 lines). Q-2 visual redesign targets this file. Props interface at lines 61-66 must remain unchanged.

Card wrapper confirmed present. Structure: <Card> → <CardHeader> → <CardTitle>Health questionnaire</CardTitle> → <CardDescription>...</CardDescription> → </CardHeader> → <CardContent> → <QuestionnaireForm onSubmit={handleQuestionnaireFromUpload} isLoading={isAnalyzing} /> → </CardContent> → </Card>. Q-2 must replace this with a direct <QuestionnaireForm ... /> render. The green confirmation Card at lines 460-472 (uploadStatus === 'questionnaire') is a separate element above the form and must be left untouched.
Background spec doc exists and is complete. Contains exact Tailwind class strings, font loading code, glow shadow values, per-element implementation specs for every surface changed in this sprint.

changes.status: MANDATORY — Cursor must act on this before writing any code
changes.detail: The prompt at lines 17-18 says 'Claude already understands the design intent and prior research for this redesign. Use that design intent directly.' Cursor has no session context. This is the IDENTICAL gap that caused Q-1 to produce generic visual output — directional language without implementation specifics, and no pointer to the spec. The full implementation spec exists at docs/QUESTIONNAIRE_VISUAL_REDESIGN_Q2_BACKGROUND.md. Cursor MUST read that file in full before implementing any visual change. The doc specifies: exact Tailwind classes for pill selected/unselected states (solid bg-primary fill, not tint); exact glow shadow values for Begin and Unlock CTAs; DM_Serif_Display font loading code; sidebar active/done/pending class strings; slider layout replacing the muted box; question numbering implementation; section card hover treatment; and the radial gradient overlay for the intent screen. Not reading this document risks a repeat of Q-1's generic output.

Prompt explicitly names all Q-1 behavioural surfaces that must not regress: props interface, flat Record<string,unknown> payload, conditionalDisplay/dependsOn logic, section order/names/estimates, ?autofill=true, schema loading path, loading/error states, validation behaviour, section-by-section flow, intent screen flow, upload-page integration. AC 1-12 from Q-1 remain in force. All are functional surfaces — visual changes must not disturb them.

Design system verified. Dark mode: bg-background = hsl(220 13% 8%), bg-card = hsl(220 13% 12%), primary = hsl(142 76% 46%). Custom palettes available: forest-*, sage-*, ocean-*. Animation keyframes available: fade-up, scale-in (in tailwind.config.ts:121-144). Primary glow token: --primary-glow = hsl(142 76% 56%). Cursor should use hsl(var(--primary)) syntax or the named Tailwind tokens rather than hardcoded hex values, so the design system remains coherent in both light and dark mode.

Upload page imports Card, CardContent, CardDescription, CardHeader, CardTitle from '@/components/ui/card' at line 4. After removing the questionnaire wrapper, check whether Card is still used elsewhere on the page (it is — the markers confirmation banner at line 474 area uses Card). CardHeader, CardTitle, CardDescription, CardContent for the questionnaire wrapper can be removed from the import if no longer used. Do not remove Card itself.

DM_Serif_Display loaded via next/font/google is a Next.js 14 built-in feature requiring no npm install. STOP condition 2 correctly handles the case where the import fails. If it does fail, the fallback is to use a CSS font-stack serif (Georgia, 'Times New Roman', serif) for the headline only — this preserves the typographic hierarchy intent without a build dependency. Do not silently drop the display font; report the STOP.

determinism_risks: Visual-only sprint: no analytical logic, no SSOT, no pipeline. Determinism risk is minimal.
State shape risk: visual restructuring must not introduce nested response objects. Flat responses map must be preserved.
Animation risk: staggered animation-delay uses inline styles — acceptable, no new dependencies.
